summary: The accessible survey page demonstrates a high level of compliance with WCAG 2.1 guidelines, effectively addressing common accessibility barriers. Minor issues remain that, if corrected, would further enhance accessibility.
strengths: Proper association of form labels with inputs
top_priority_fixes: Ensure all form elements have appropriate labels
Provide text alternatives for all non-text content
Verify sufficient color contrast for all text elements

issues.description: The image lacks a descriptive text alternative, which is essential for users relying on screen readers.
issues.recommendation: Add a descriptive alt attribute to the image, such as 'Weather forecast: Sunny Spells'.

issues.description: The color contrast between the navigation menu text and background may not meet the minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1, potentially making it difficult for users with low vision to read.
issues.recommendation: Adjust the text color or background color to achieve a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1.

issues.description: The dropdown lacks a visible label, which can cause confusion for users, especially those using assistive technologies.
issues.recommendation: Ensure the dropdown has a visible and descriptive label, such as 'Select the greenest city'.
